summary: This is an ultra-brief link post where Joel Spolsky shares that Ev (Evan Williams, creator of Blogger) reviewed Joel's 'User Interface for Programmers' series. The entire post is a single sentence with a thank-you. It serves as a social acknowledgment and cross-blog reference typical of early 2000s blogging culture.
key_points: Joel shares a link to Evan Williams' review of his UI for Programmers series
The post exemplifies the early blogosphere's culture of mutual cross-linking and acknowledgment
At just 6 words of original content, it represents the micro-post format common in early blogs
key_insight: A minimal link post reflecting the early blogging culture of cross-referencing and community engagement among tech bloggers.
